title: Labeling lysosomes in the adult Drosophila brain using LysoTracker
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ekqtn","text":"This protocol describes the labeling and quantification of Lysosomes in the adult Drosophila brain using the LysoTracker dye","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":82,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the working solution, 0.05 μM LysoTracker Green DND26, using 1X PBS
Brains from 10-day-old animals are dissected in ice-cold 1X PBS
The dissected brain is transferred to a glass slide, and the excess 1X PBS removed
The brains are re-incubated with 0.05 μM LysoTracker for 00:05:00 at Room temperature
After 5 minutes, the excess solution is removed, a drop of DAPI Fluoromount added, and the brain mounted with a coverslip
Brains re imaged immediately using a Zeiss confocal microscope under a 63X objective lens
The number of puncta per 1000 μm 2 is counted in Zen software
creator: Beatrice Weykopf
